commit.message:
fix: 5 nice-to-have audit fixes — UTF-8 safety, dedup stage key, atomic context.json

UTF-8 truncation safety (BUG-A06, BUG-C07, BUG-D10):
- output.go NormalizeCode: use []rune for prev-char lookup
- extract.go tail(): use []rune for safe multi-byte truncation
- history.go and swarm_executor.go: use []rune for summary truncation

Deduplicate stage key function (BUG-D08):
- Add SwarmStage.Key() method on compile.SwarmStage
- Replace identical swarmStageName() and parallelStageKey() with
  delegating one-liners

Atomic context.json writes (BUG-C04):
- Write to .tmp then os.Rename for crash-safe context generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Mac版: スクショ即保存・多重起動防止を追加

- fcntl.flockによる多重起動防止を追加

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: full responsive audit + lightbox on document review

- Made all 44+ pages responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop
- Fixed inline grid layouts to use Tailwind responsive breakpoints
- Wrapped all tables in overflow-x-auto with min-w constraints
- Added flex-wrap to button groups and overflow-x-auto to tab bars
- Fixed fixed viewport heights to only apply at lg: breakpoint
- AdminDocumentReview: two-panel grid now collapses to single column below xl
- AdminDocumentReview: added lightbox viewer (DocumentPreviewModal) on each doc row
- Updated nav layouts (ControlCentre, SupplierHub, ClientDashboard) for mobile

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: remove all mock systems + add production safety & Firebase cost optimization

Phase 1-3: Remove MockCamera, MockPrinter, generateMockImages, and all
'mock' options from config types, Settings UI, and PreEntryPage.
Default camera changed to 'edsdk', printer to 'system'.

Phase 4: Add ErrorBoundary component to catch render errors in kiosk mode
with restart button — prevents blank white screen during events.

Phase 5: Daily stats aggregation (hourly→daily cron at 2AM),
image resize reduced to 1600px/0.7 quality (~400KB/photo),
stats_live single document for dashboard (1 read vs 24).

Phase 6: Event lock (reject uploads after event ended),
upload rate limit (3s cooldown per guest, in-memory map).

Phase 7: Lucky draw O(1) random index (random field on guest docs),
concurrent draw lock (drawLock field), stats_live on-write trigger.

Firebase cost estimate: ~$0.50/event for 10k users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: change PREVIEW_PORT from 4300 to 8080

The NixOS VM firewall (nixos-fw chain) only allows incoming TCP on ports
22, 80, 443, 4200, and 8080. Port 4300 was never in the allowlist, so
the preview pane always showed "No preview" even when a dev server was
running on port 4300.

Port 8080 is already allowed in the VM firewall and unoccupied, making
it the correct port for preview dev servers. Also adds build-container.nix
for reproducible container builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: doable

commit.message:
fix: code review improvements across controller, display, and server (#22)

* fix: code review improvements across controller, display, and server

- Fix null roomCode bug in showRoomGone() when no room code in URL
- Include results in WELCOME message so controllers restore results on reconnect
- Add per-IP rate limiting (30 req/10s) to /api/qr endpoint
- Replace fragile hex color parsing with color-mix() in display renderResults
- Use const in for...of loops across display files for consistency
- Remove unnecessary compassHints null checks (element always in DOM)
- Remove redundant stopDisplayGame() call in RESULTS peer-left path
- Remove dead lobbyTitle variable from ControllerState
- Add IIFE load order documentation and drag direction comment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use X-Forwarded-For for rate limit client IP

Behind Traefik, req.socket.remoteAddress is always the proxy pod IP.
Read X-Forwarded-For first hop to get the actual client IP.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert: remove QR rate limiting

Rate limiting doesn't work properly behind Traefik (proxy IP issue).
Client-side QR generation is the better long-term fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review feedback — comments, const consistency

- Add comment explaining compassHints DOM invariant
- Add comment for results reconnect fallback path
- Add comment clarifying lastResults.results structure
- Use const for loop body destructures in display for...of loops

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove broken IIFE split across controller script files

Each <script> tag is parsed as an independent program by the browser,
so opening (function(){) in ControllerState.js and closing })() in
controller.js causes SyntaxError in both files.

Remove the IIFE and use plain global scope (matching the display-side
pattern). Replace the early return with an if/else guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: rename var to avoid shadowing in results reconnect block

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: MLB Sprint 4 fully deployed — scheduler jobs + BDL migration + handoff

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: guard try_call_and_deserialize against zero-address calls (#44)

call_contract_syscall to address 0x0 now causes a full revert in the
current Starknet VM instead of returning Result::Err. This broke
token_uri for games that don't implement optional components like
settings or objectives — the settings_address would resolve to 0x0
and the subsequent settings_details call would revert the entire
token_uri.

Add a zero-address early return in try_call_and_deserialize so all
call sites are protected. Also add an upgrade_denshokan.sh script
to apply the fix to the deployed contract.

Co-authored-by: starknetdev <starknetdev@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: escape JS object literal in broadcast template to avoid Jinja2 parse error

${{ was being parsed as a Jinja2 expression block, causing TemplateSyntaxError
on every GET /broadcast request. Changed to ${ { (space added) which is valid
JS template literal syntax and invisible to Jinja2.

commit.message:
docs: add performance profiling results and update CLAUDE.md (#191) (#195)

Add docs/reference/PERFORMANCE_PROFILING.md with full experiment data:
- Per-property IPC cost: ~17ms (the root cause of all slowness)
- `whose` clauses: 20-30x faster than manual iteration
- String concatenation: NOT a bottleneck (<100ms for 400 items)
- Projected optimization: get_tasks(flagged) from 18.9s to ~3.5s

Update CLAUDE.md performance section with corrected understanding
based on empirical profiling rather than untested hypotheses.

commit.message:
fix: Maestro CI — add pipefail, show build errors, pass empty Sentry token

The xcodebuild step was piped through `tail -50` which:
1. Hid the actual build error (only showed last 50 lines)
2. Swallowed the exit code (no pipefail)

Now: tee to file, show last 200 lines, grep errors on failure.

commit.message:
feat: add naturgy-agent-functional-doc and naturgy-agent-orchestrator

naturgy-agent-functional-doc: Analista funcional — genera la SSOT
- Transforma cualquier input de negocio (texto, PDF, notas) en specs completas
- Produce _data/specs/: functional-spec, domain-model, data-spec, api-spec,
  ui-flows, nfr-spec, acceptance-criteria, clarifications
- Es el primer agente en ejecutarse; todos los demás leen su output
- Incluye wireframes ASCII, DTOs completos, criterios verificables y supuestos

naturgy-agent-orchestrator: Director de orquesta del ecosistema
- Lee _data/specs/ y evalúa el estado del proyecto
- Diseña el plan de ejecución con fases, dependencias y paralelismos
- Mantiene EXECUTION_PLAN.md actualizado en tiempo real
- Gestiona bloqueos de seguridad y arquitectura
- Replanifica automáticamente ante fallos

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sprint 128: QA audit — fix missing warp, correct encounter data, clean warnings, add 5 tests

Fixed SlowpokeWellB1F missing warp to B2F per pokecrystal warp_event data.
Corrected encounter tables for BurnedTowerB1F (removed RATICATE/MAGMAR),
UnionCaveB1F (removed SANDSHREW), and UnionCaveB2F (removed GRAVELER) to
match pokecrystal wild data. Fixed 8 compiler warnings across 7 files.
Added 5 new QA tests covering Slowpoke Well Rocket event, Burned Tower
beast encounter, Ilex Forest Farfetch'd quest, Union Cave floor traversal,
and bidirectional warp connectivity. 1361 tests passing, 0 warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: suppress Sentry noise for DoltHub token permission errors

The DOLTHUB_TOKEN lacks SQL permissions on hop/wl-commons, causing
handleBrowse and handleDashboard to capture the same config error to
Sentry on every request (93+ events). Adds isTokenPermissionError
classifier to suppress captures and return a specific error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Shift+letter bug in editor, clean up divedit.c

SDL2 TEXTINPUT fix (divmouse.c): SDL_TEXTINPUT was setting scan_code
to the ASCII value of the typed character. ASCII 'M'=77 collides with
DOS scan code 77 (Right Arrow), 'P'=80 with Down, 'Q'=81 with PgDn,
so Shift+M/P/Q triggered navigation instead of typing uppercase.
Fix: only set ascii from TEXTINPUT, not scan_code.

divedit.c cleanups:
- Restored original .PRG extension check for syntax colorizer (Mike
  commented it out with inverted logic)
- Fixed f_cortar_bloque(memptrsize) back to f_cortar_bloque(int) —
  parameter is a 0/1/2 enum, not a pointer
- Removed 3 dead #ifdef TTF blocks (TTF is disabled on all platforms):
  SDL_FillRect call, SDL_FreeSurface in resize_surface, and the
  put_char3 TTF rendering path (which had a bug: (char*)c vs (char)c)

commit.message:
fix: make migration 0000 idempotent for production

Added IF NOT EXISTS to CREATE TABLE/INDEX and wrapped ALTER TABLE
ADD CONSTRAINT in DO blocks with duplicate_object exception handling.
This allows drizzle-kit migrate to safely run on PROD where tables
already exist, enabling migrations 0030/0031 to be applied.

commit.message:
Instant client-side tab switching for hub sidebar

Eliminate ~1s delay when switching between Parties/Progress/Settings tabs.
Previously every click triggered a full server round-trip through middleware
(2 Supabase network calls). Now uses window.history.pushState for zero-latency
client-side navigation with popstate handling for back/forward.

Also caches the middleware onboarding check in a cookie to speed up remaining
server navigations (direct URLs, party detail pages).
